title: COOLING ELEMENTS AND COOLING ASSEMBLIES INCLUDING THE SAME
abstract: A cooling element for cooling a body, comprising a heat conduction layer coterminous with a proximal side of the cooling element, a heat retardant layer coterminous with a distal side of the cooling element, and a heat sink volume disposed between the heat conduction and heat retardant layers, extending from a proximal boundary with the heat conduction layer to a distal boundary with the heat retardant layer. The heat sink volume comprises a porous material including a first substance; and the heat conduction layer comprises a porous material including a second substance. The first and second substances have thermal properties such that the first substance will solidify at a first temperature less than 20Â°C, the second substance being in the liquid state at the first temperature. The heat retardant layer has a lower mean thermal conductivity than the heat conduction layer. The cooling element is configured such that when the proximal side of the cooling element contacts a surface of the body as in use, the heat conduction layer will conduct heat from the body and into the heat sink volume more rapidly than heat is conducted through the heat retardant layer and into the heat sink volume, from the distal side of the cooling element.
claims:
Claims 1. A cooling element, comprising a heat conduction layer coterminous with a proximal side of the cooling element, a heat retardant layer coterminous with a distal side of the cooling element, and a heat sink volume disposed between the heat conduction and heat retardant layers, extending from a proximal boundary with the heat conduction layer to a distal boundary with the heat retardant layer; in which the heat sink volume comprises a porous material including a first substance; the heat conduction layer comprises a porous material including a second substance; the first and second substances having thermal properties such that the first substance will solidify at a first temperature less than 20Â°C, the second substance being in the liquid state at the first temperature; and the heat retardant layer has a lower mean thermal conductivity than the heat conduction layer; configured such that when the proximal side of the cooling element contacts a surface of the body as in use, the heat conduction layer will conduct heat from the body and into the heat sink volume more rapidly than heat is conducted through the heat retardant layer and into the heat sink volume, from the distal side of the cooling element. 2. A cooling element as claimed in claim 1 for cooling a body, in which the cooling element is sufficiently flexible to be capable of curving in any of a range of arcs, in response to being placed against a curved surface of the body. 3. A cooling element as claimed in claim 1 or claim 2, in which the heat sink volume comprises a plurality of heat sink elements. A cooling element as claimed in any of the preceding claims, in which the porous material of the heat sink volume comprises a biodegradable and/or compostable material. A cooling element as claimed in any of the preceding claims, in which the porous material of the conduction layer is a biodegradable and/or compostable material. A cooling element as claimed in any of the preceding claims, in which the heat retardant layer is a biodegradable and/or compostable material. A cooling element as claimed in any of claims 4 to 6, in which the porous material of the heat conduction layer, and the porous material of the heat sink volume, and the heat retardant layer are biodegradable and / or compostable. 8. A cooling element as claimed in any of claims 4 to 7, in which the biodegradable and/or compostable material comprises at least one of paper, cardboard, hemp fibres, bamboo fibres, or wood-pulp material. 9. A cooling element as claimed in any of the preceding claims, in which the volume of the second substance is less than the volume of the first substance. 10. A cooling element as claimed in any of the preceding claims, in which the heat retardant layer is free of any liquid phase up to 50Â°C. 11. A cooling element as claimed in any of the preceding claims, in which the first substance comprises water. 12. A cooling element as claimed in any of the preceding claims, in which the second substance comprises an aqueous solution. 13. A cooling element as claimed in claim 12, in which the second substance comprise a solution of sodium chloride in water. 14. A cooling element as claimed in any of the preceding claims, in which the mean thickness of heat conduction layer is 0.5 - 3 mm. 15. A cooling element as claimed in any of the preceding claims, in which the mean thickness of heat retardant layer is 1 - 15 mm. 16. A cooling element as claimed in any of the preceding claims, in which the pores of the porous material of the heat conduction layer are filled with the second substance; or partly filled and include air voids. 17. A cooling assembly for cooling a body comprising one or more cooling element as claimed in any of the preceding claims, and a container for containing the body and the cooling element. 18. A cooling assembly as claimed in claim 17, comprising a plurality of cooling elements. 19. A cooling assembly as claimed in claim 17 or 18, in which the container is configured for accommodating more than one body. 20. A method of cooling a body, the method including providing a cooling assembly as claimed in any of claims 1 to 16; reducing the temperature of the heat sink volume to less than the first temperature; arranging the cooling element between the body and a surface of the container, the body being housed within the container; the proximal side of the cooling element being in contact with the body. 21. A method as claimed in claim 20, including 

 providing a cooling element such that the heat capacity of the heat sink volume is sufficiently great to reduce the temperature of the body by at least 5Â°C, from an initial temperature of around 20Â°C to 40Â°C.
